docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
policy-meta-spec is the canonical semantic contract for policy metadata shared by:

  • agent-exec-gateway
  • safe-fs-tools
  • omne-agent

It defines stable naming, strict canonical values, alias normalization rules, baseline profiles, and machine-readable schema artifacts.

docs.title: Why This Spec Exists
docs.text:
Distributed agent systems often diverge on policy token naming and meaning. Once divergence happens, interoperability and auditability break down quickly.

This repository is the single source of truth for policy metadata semantics.

docs.title: Audience
docs.text:
This docs set is designed for all three consumers:

  • Humans: conceptual clarity and migration guidance.
  • Agents: deterministic tokens and strict normalization rules.
  • Tools: schema contract and versioned compatibility expectations.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
In scope:

  • canonical field names and enum values,
  • parse-time alias handling,
  • schema-level interoperability contract,
  • baseline profile presets,
  • migration and stability rules.

Out of scope:

  • runtime policy engine internals,
  • transport API definitions,
  • platform-specific sandbox implementation details.

docs.title: Start Here
docs.text:
1. Quickstart
  2. Core Model
  3. Risk Profiles
  4. Schema Reference

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
This section gives a fast adoption path for policy-meta-spec.

Recommended order:

  1. Quickstart
  2. Core Model
  3. Alias and Normalization
  4. Schema Reference

docs.title: 1. Use Canonical Fields
docs.text:
v1 canonical fields:

  • risk_profile
  • write_scope
  • execution_isolation
  • decision

docs.title: 2. Create a Canonical Policy Object
docs.text:
version: 1
risk_profile: standard
write_scope: workspace_write
execution_isolation: best_effort
decision: prompt

docs.title: 3. Validate with JSON Schema
docs.text:
Use schema/policy-meta.v1.json in CI and integration tests.

npx ajv validate -s schema/policy-meta.v1.json -d policy.json

docs.title: 4. Normalize Aliases at Parse Boundary Only
docs.text:
Accepted parse aliases:

  • yolo -> danger
  • workspace -> workspace_write
  • global -> full_access
  • unrestricted -> full_access

Persist and emit canonical values only.

docs.title: 5. Fail Closed on Unknown Values
docs.text: Unknown enum values or unknown object properties should be rejected.

docs.title: 6. Verify Profile Defaults
docs.text:
Baseline profile files are in profiles/:

  • safe.yaml
  • standard.yaml
  • proactive.yaml
  • danger.yaml

docs.title: Guides
docs.text:
Detailed semantic and migration guidance.

  • Core Model
  • Risk Profiles
  • Alias and Normalization
  • Migration to v1
  • AI and Agent Consumption

docs.title: AI and Agent Consumption
docs.text: AI/agent systems benefit from strict and explicit policy token contracts.

docs.title: Integration Pattern
docs.text:
human or system input
-> parse aliases
-> canonical object
-> schema validation
-> runtime policy evaluation
-> structured audit output

docs.title: Recommendations
docs.text:
• pass canonical values to model/tool context,
  • include explicit spec version,
  • reject free-form synonyms not in canonical/alias tables,
  • log raw token and normalized token for traceability.

docs.title: Error Message Style
docs.text:
Use strict corrective messages.

Example:

Invalid write_scope: workspace
Use canonical value: workspace_write

docs.title: Safe Defaults for Autonomous Contexts
docs.text:
• prefer safe or standard,
  • avoid full_access without explicit escalation,
  • require intentional transitions to higher-risk profiles.

docs.title: Alias and Normalization
docs.text: v1 allows a minimal alias set for parse-time ergonomics.

docs.title: Alias Table
docs.text: Field Alias Canonical risk_profile yolo danger write_scope workspace workspace_write write_scope global full_access write_scope unrestricted full_access

docs.title: Required Behavior
docs.text:
• parsers may accept documented aliases,
  • parsers must normalize aliases immediately,
  • serializers must emit canonical values only,
  • persisted objects must not contain alias tokens,
  • unknown values must fail closed.

docs.title: Recommended Pipeline
docs.text: input -> parse -> alias map -> enum validation -> canonical object -> persistence

docs.title: Why Boundary-Only Aliases
docs.text: Alias tokens inside storage create semantic drift, inconsistent querying, and migration complexity.

docs.title: Canonical Fields
docs.text: Field Allowed Values Purpose risk_profile safe, standard, proactive, danger High-level risk posture label. write_scope read_only, workspace_write, full_access Filesystem write reach intent. execution_isolation none, best_effort, strict Strength of execution boundary guarantees. decision allow, prompt, prompt_strict, deny User/agent approval behavior.

docs.title: risk_profile
docs.text:
Represents operational risk posture:

  • safe: conservative restrictions.
  • standard: balanced default.
  • proactive: more autonomy with guardrails.
  • danger: highest-risk posture.

docs.title: write_scope
docs.text:
Represents intended write scope:

  • read_only
  • workspace_write
  • full_access

workspace_write and full_access are intentionally explicit for auditability.

docs.title: execution_isolation
docs.text:
Represents guarantee strength, not backend name:

  • none
  • best_effort
  • strict

docs.title: decision
docs.text:
Represents approval behavior:

  • allow
  • prompt
  • prompt_strict
  • deny

docs.title: Normative Rules
docs.text:
• canonical meanings are immutable,
  • aliases are parse-time compatibility only,
  • persisted values must remain canonical,
  • unknown values must fail closed.

docs.title: Migration to v1
docs.text: Migration to v1 is intentionally breaking to establish consistent canonical naming.

docs.title: Canonical Renames
docs.text:
• workspace_best_effort -> best_effort
  • workspace_strict -> strict
  • danger_full_access -> full_access

docs.title: Parse-Time Compatibility Aliases
docs.text:
• yolo -> danger
  • workspace -> workspace_write
  • global -> full_access
  • unrestricted -> full_access

docs.title: Recommended Rollout
docs.text:
1. Accept legacy tokens and aliases at boundary with warnings.
  2. Rewrite stored records to canonical v1 values.
  3. Enforce strict canonical output and reject unknowns.

docs.title: Migration Checklist
docs.text:
• enumerate every storage location with policy values,
  • apply deterministic mapping,
  • validate rewritten records,
  • ensure all services share one normalization table.

docs.title: Risk Profiles
docs.text: Preset profiles are stored in profiles/*.yaml.

docs.title: Built-In Presets
docs.text: Profile risk_profile write_scope execution_isolation safe safe read_only strict standard standard workspace_write best_effort proactive proactive workspace_write best_effort danger danger full_access none

docs.title: Selection Guidance
docs.text:
• safe: compliance-sensitive and untrusted-input-heavy contexts.
  • standard: typical engineering default.
  • proactive: semi-autonomous workflows with oversight.
  • danger: explicitly authorized high-risk scenarios.

docs.title: Operational Recommendation
docs.text: Start at standard, measure friction and risk, then escalate scope intentionally.

docs.title: Decision Pairing (Guideline)
docs.text:
Profile Common decision safe prompt_strict or deny standard prompt proactive prompt or allow danger allow

These pairings are guidance, not schema constraints.

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
Machine-readable contracts and compatibility rules.

  • Schema Reference
  • Rust Reference
  • Governance and Versioning

docs.title: Stability Rules
docs.text:
• existing canonical values are immutable in meaning,
  • backward-incompatible rename/removal requires major version bump,
  • additive evolution is preferred.

docs.title: Change Categories
docs.text: Change Compatibility Typical release action New field additive minor release + docs update New enum value additive but behavior-sensitive minor release + integration notice Rename/removal breaking major version + migration guide New alias parse-time compatibility enhancement minor release

docs.title: Consumer Guidance
docs.text:
• pin to a known spec version in production,
  • validate upgrades with real traces,
  • keep rollback path available.

docs.title: Rust Reference
docs.text: Rust crate path: rust/policy-meta/

docs.title: Enums
docs.text:
• RiskProfile
  • WriteScope
  • ExecutionIsolation
  • Decision

All use serde(rename_all = "snake_case") to preserve canonical wire tokens.

docs.title: Alias Support in Serde
docs.text:
• RiskProfile::Danger accepts yolo
  • WriteScope::WorkspaceWrite accepts workspace
  • WriteScope::FullAccess accepts global, unrestricted

Serialization remains canonical.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
use policy_meta::{RiskProfile, WriteScope};

let risk: RiskProfile = serde_json::from_str("\"yolo\"")?;
assert_eq!(risk, RiskProfile::Danger);

let scope = WriteScope::FullAccess;
assert_eq!(serde_json::to_string(&scope)?, "\"full_access\"");
# Ok::<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>>(())

docs.title: Integration Guidance
docs.text:
1. parse with alias support,
  2. normalize to canonical enums,
  3. persist canonical values only,
  4. evaluate runtime logic on canonical enums.

docs.title: Schema Reference
docs.text: Canonical schema file: schema/policy-meta.v1.json

docs.title: Schema Identity
docs.text:
{
  "$schema": "https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema",
  "$id": "https://omne42.dev/schema/policy-meta.v1.json",
  "title": "PolicyMetaV1"
}

docs.title: Top-Level Rules
docs.text:
• type: object
  • additionalProperties: false

Unknown keys are invalid.

docs.title: Enum Domains
docs.text:
• risk_profile: safe | standard | proactive | danger
  • write_scope: read_only | workspace_write | full_access
  • execution_isolation: none | best_effort | strict
  • decision: allow | prompt | prompt_strict | deny

docs.title: Important Note
docs.text:
Aliases are parser behavior, not schema enum values.

Example: workspace is accepted at parse boundary by some implementations but is invalid under canonical schema enums.
